profile.behavior: Soars with wings held in a shallow V, often rocking side to side; rides thermals with few wingbeats and searches for carrion chiefly by smell.
field_marks: Large, dark brown-black vulture with a small, bare head.
Adults show a red, wrinkled head and a pale hooked bill.
Long, broad wings have conspicuously fingered tips.
In flight, the wings are held in a shallow V (dihedral).
Underwings are two-toned: dark leading edge and paler silvery trailing flight feathers.
Long tail extends beyond the toe tips in flight.
profile.habitat: Open and semi-open country, including mixed farmland, rangeland, forest edges, and other landscapes with carrion; roosts in secluded trees, cliffs, rocks, and similar elevated sites.

sources.title: Turkey Vulture Identification — Cornell Lab of Ornithology
Turkey Vulture Life History — Cornell Lab of Ornithology
Turkey Vulture — University of Michigan BioKIDS

findings: No material anatomical, species, text, or location-detail errors found. The illustration depicts one head, one hooked bill, two wings, two legs, and one long tail; adult bare red head, pale bill, dark-brown plumage, pinkish legs, broad fingered wings, and two-toned underwing presentation agree with the supplied references and verification sources.

All visible factual claims are supported: Turkey Vulture / Cathartes aura / Cathartidae; 64–81 cm length, 0.85–2.0 kg weight, and 170–183 cm wingspan; shallow-V soaring, side-to-side rocking, thermal use, carrion detection chiefly by smell, broad open-to-semi-open habitat, and elevated secluded roosting sites.
